import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

y = df['Chance of Admit ']

X.shape
Out[16]:

In [17]:

In [18]:
X=np.array(X)
y=np.array(y)
In [19]:
y=y.reshape(-1,1)
In [20]:
y.shape
Out[20]:
(500, 1)
In [21]:
# scaling the data before training the model
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, MinMaxScaler
scaler_x = StandardScaler()
X=scaler_x.fit_transform(X)
In [22]:
scaler_y = StandardScaler()
y=scaler_y.fit_transform(y)
In [23]:
# splitting the data into test and train sets
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X,y, test_size=0.15)
In [24]:
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
randomForest_model = RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators=100, max_depth=10)
randomForest_model.fit(X_train, y_train)

<ipython-input-24-b46cdefa77dc>:3: DataConversionWarning: A column-vector y was passed when a 1d array was expected. Please change the shape of y to (n_samples,), for example using ravel().
  randomForest_model.fit(X_train, y_train)
Out[24]:
RandomForestRegressor(max_depth=10)
In [25]:
accuracy_randomforest = randomForest_model.score(X_test, y_test)
accuracy_randomforest
Out[25]:
0.833410700025925
In [26]:
RDF_regressor = RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators = 30, random_state = 0)
RDF_regressor.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred2=RDF_regressor.predict(X_test)
y_train_pred2=RDF_regressor.predict(X_train)

<ipython-input-26-cd53613a7ba1>:2: DataConversionWarning: A column-vector y was passed when a 1d array was expected. Please change the shape of y to (n_samples,), for example using ravel().
  RDF_regressor.fit(X_train, y_train)
In [27]:
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error
from math import sqrt
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score
print("MSE score on the test set:",sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, y_pred2)))
print("MSE score on the training set:",sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train, y_train_pred2)))

print("RMSE score on the test set:",sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_test, y_pred2)))
print("RMSE score on the training set:",sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_train, y_train_pred2)))

print("R2 score on the test set:",r2_score(y_test, y_pred2)*100)
print("R2 score on the training set:",r2_score(y_train, y_train_pred2)*100)

MSE score on the test set: 0.4424852513390954
MSE score on the training set: 0.17918557472664784
RMSE score on the test set: 0.4424852513390954
RMSE score on the training set: 0.17918557472664784
R2 score on the test set: 82.15967978489367
R2 score on the training set: 96.73257065180852
In [28]:
from sklearn import decomposition
pca=decomposition.PCA()
In [29]:
pca.n_components=2
pca_data=pca.fit_transform(X)
print("The reduced shape is", pca_data.shape)

The reduced shape is (500, 2)
In [30]:
pca_data[:5]
Out[30]:
array([[-3.56837458, -0.32831807],
       [-1.6391986 ,  0.03455037],
       [ 0.55099145, -0.72079379],
       [-0.34547019, -1.28023739],
       [ 2.03580394,  0.03936553]])
In [31]:
pca_data=np.vstack((pca_data.T,new_labels)).T
pca_df=pd.DataFrame(data=pca_data,columns=("Principal Component 1","Principal Component 2","Chances of Admit"))
pca_df.head()
Out[31]:
Principal Component 1 Principal Component 2 Chances of Admit
0 -3.568375 -0.328318 good
1 -1.639199 0.03455 medium
2 0.550991 -0.720794 medium
3 -0.34547 -1.280237 good
4 2.035804 0.039366 medium
In [32]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
# the size of A4 paper
fig.set_size_inches(12, 8)
sns.scatterplot(x="Principal Component 1", y="Principal Component 2", hue="Chances of Admit", data=pca_df);
